source_jobs.title: Associate Director/Director Business Development
source_jobs.description:
Location: London (Hybrid Working)

 

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

 

Role Purpose

As our Associate Director/Director Business Development, you'll be a senior commercial force in the life sciences market - leading high-value, strategic new business for Mystra, our SaaS data and analytics platform, as well as our technology-enabled consultancy business. You'll engage at the most senior levels of pharma and biotech, building the kind of relationships and deals that define Genomics' trajectory.

This is a role for someone with deep commercial instincts, a genuine scientific grounding, and the confidence to operate as a trusted peer to R&D and data leaders at the world's leading pharmaceutical companies. You'll be a key senior individual contributor in a fast-moving Commercial team, and your success will directly shape the future of the business.

 

A Day in the Life

Strategic New Business: You'll own and drive the most significant new commercial opportunities across pharma and biotech - building multi-stakeholder relationships at senior and executive level and leading complex, high-value sales cycles from first conversation to contract close.

Executive Client Relationships: You'll invest deeply in understanding your clients' long-term R&D and data strategies, positioning Mystra as an essential part of how they work - not just a vendor, but a scientific partner.

Market Leadership: You'll bring a sophisticated view of the life sciences landscape, feeding strategic intelligence back into our go-to-market approach and influencing how we position and evolve our platform.

Sales Operations: You'll set a high standard for pipeline discipline and forecasting, contributing to strategic reporting that informs senior decision-making across the business.

Internal Influence: You'll carry significant internal weight as the voice of the customer — shaping how Product, Science, and Marketing think about the market and ensuring our commercial approach is always grounded in genuine value.

 

Who You Are

 - You have a substantial track record of senior-level SaaS, digital health, or enterprise technology sales into pharma or biotech, with demonstrable success closing complex, high-value deals.

 - You have deep experience engaging with R&D and research departments inside major pharmaceutical companies, and you navigate senior scientific and commercial stakeholders with ease.

 - You have a strong working knowledge of genetics or genomics — enough to speak credibly and compellingly about what advanced genetic analytics can unlock for drug discovery.

 - You think strategically about markets, clients, and positioning, and bring that perspective into everything you do.

 - You're a self-directed, senior operator who thrives in a fast-paced scale-up environment and doesn't need a roadmap to find the right path.

 - A degree in life sciences, business, or a related field is expected; an advanced degree is a genuine asset.

 

Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.


COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward,

source_jobs.title: Senior Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Location: Oxford or London (Hybrid)



The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.



A Day in the Life

As a Senior Scientist, you'll drive innovation and collaboration, acting as a strategic consultant on high-impact projects with diverse external pharma and biotech partners. You’ll communicate insights effectively, helping to shape team direction and leading transformative initiatives across the discovery and development spectrum.

You will navigate across the entirety of the drug development pipeline, from target discovery and validation through to patient stratification. Utilizing a strategic approach and advanced statistical techniques, you will work closely with external collaborators to prioritize therapeutic targets, optimize clinical insights, and unveil new treatment possibilities.

Success here demands a robust grasp of statistical human genetics applied inventively in a partner-facing environment. You'll excel in unraveling complex genomic data and possess a knack for translating raw findings into actionable consulting insights, whilst working collectively with both your highly skilled internal team and external partners.



Who You Are

 - Experience in performing foundational genetic association analyses (e.g., GWAS; PRS)

 - Strong competency in statistical programming (e.g., R, Python) sufficient to enable large-scale genomic data analysis

 - Experience in data mining and/or management of large datasets.

 - Confident in engaging with a variety of stakeholders, contributing and supporting in the delivery of presentations to customers.



Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.




COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Salary: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure top-of-market compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous Learning: Including external courses and a wide library of L&D materials, because your growth is our success.


WELLBEING & TIME OFF

 - Holiday: 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays, plus an extra 3-day company-wide shutdown at year-end.

 - Financial & Health Security: Robust benefits including a market-leading pension scheme, comprehensive private health insurance for you and your family with NO excess, critical illness, and life assurance.

 - Enhanced Leave: Enhanced paid family leave to support all new parents.


WORK ENVIRONMENT & CULTURE

 - Flexible Working: Hybrid Working (e.g., From our London, Oxford Office)

 - Truly Inclusive Time Off: Our 'Bank Your Bank Holiday' program allows you to exchange public holidays for dates that hold personal or cultural significance to you.

 - Vibrant Social Culture: From regular Town Halls and team picnics to organised sports events, our social committee ensures frequent opportunities to connect and celebrate.

 - Green Commute: Cycle-to-Work scheme and convenient office locations near major transport hubs.
   
   

Ready to Build the Future?

If this opportunity excites you, apply now!

We are dedicated to creating a diverse environment and are proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. All q

source_jobs.title: Leadership Team Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Location: London (4 days a week in office)

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.




ROLE PURPOSE

We are looking for a highly organised Leadership Team Executive Assistant to be the calm, coordinated centre of our senior leaders' working lives. Partnering closely with our Senior EA and the wider Admin Team, you will keep complex diaries, meetings, events and travel running seamlessly — the kind of behind-the-scenes excellence that frees a leadership team to focus on building the future of healthcare.




A DAY IN THE LIFE

You will juggle competing diaries across multiple leadership team members, schedule everything from client sessions to strategic working groups and away days, and coordinate offsites and high-level partner meetings down to the last detail of venue, catering and materials. You will book complex national and international travel - visas included - keep expenses accurate and on time, and handle sensitive information with complete discretion. It is fast-paced, varied and genuinely central to how the business runs.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Experienced supporting multiple senior leaders in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment, anticipating needs before they arise

 - A natural organiser who can prioritise multiple schedules and workstreams with precision, flexibility and a high degree of attention to detail

 - A skilled, professional communicator - confident with internal teams and external partners alike, in writing and in person

 - Trusted with sensitive and confidential information across executive discussions, people-related topics and strategic projects

 - Proficient across Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Slides, Sheets), Slack, Zoom and Outlook
   
   

Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.

Compensation & Growth

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous Learning: Including external courses and a wide library of L&D materials, because your growth is our success.


WELLBEING & TIME OFF

 - Holiday: 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays, plus an extra 3-day company-wide shutdown at year-end.

 - Financial & Health Security: Robust benefits including a market-leading pension scheme, comprehensive private health insurance for you and your family with NO excess, critical illness, and life assurance.

 - Enhanced Leave: Enhanced paid family leave to support all new parents.


WORK ENVIRONMENT & CULTURE

 - Hybrid Working

 - Truly Inclusive Time Off: Our 'Bank Your Bank Holiday' program allows you to exchange public holidays for dates that hold personal or cultural significance to you.

 - Vibrant Social Culture: From regular Town Halls and team picnics to organised sports events, our social committee ensures frequent opportunities to connect and celebrate.

 - Green Commute: Cycle-to-Work scheme and convenient office locations near major transport hubs.

Ready to Build the Future?

If this opportunity excites you, apply now!

source_jobs.title: Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Location: Oxford or London (Hybrid)

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

A Day in the Life

As a Scientist, you'll drive innovation and collaboration, acting as a strategic consultant on high-impact projects with diverse external pharma and biotech partners. You’ll communicate insights effectively, helping to shape team direction and leading transformative initiatives across the discovery and development spectrum.

You will navigate across the entirety of the drug development pipeline, from target discovery and validation through to patient stratification. Utilizing a strategic approach and advanced statistical techniques, you will work closely with external collaborators to prioritize therapeutic targets, optimize clinical insights, and unveil new treatment possibilities.

Success here demands a robust grasp of statistical human genetics applied inventively in a partner-facing environment. You'll excel in unraveling complex genomic data and possess a knack for translating raw findings into actionable consulting insights, whilst working collectively with both your highly skilled internal team and external partners.

Who You Are

 - Experience performing foundational genetic association analyses (e.g. GWAS, burden) on large-scale biobank data in Trusted Research Environments (TREs) such as UK Biobank and All of Us.

 - Strong proficiency in R and/or Python for large-scale genomic analysis, with a commitment to clean, reproducible, well-documented code.

 - Experience with workflow tools (e.g. Nextflow), version control (Git), and HPC or cloud compute environments.

 - Nice to have: containerisation (Docker/Singularity), distributed computing frameworks, machine learning applied to genomics, or contributions to open-source scientific software.

 - Confident in engaging with a variety of stakeholders, contributing and supporting in the delivery of presentations to customers.

Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: Hybrid from our RTP office OR Fully Remote based on the East Coast



The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.



Role Purpose

As our Business Development Lead, you'll be a driven, scientifically-minded commercial professional helping to grow Mystra’s penetration into the pharma and biotech market. You'll own your pipeline, build real relationships with client R&D and data teams, and play a hands-on role in selling Mystra and our specialised consultancy services.

Operating in one of the most exciting growth areas in life sciences, this is a brilliant opportunity for someone with commercial ambition who is ready to take genuine ownership. You will help clients solve complex challenges through a mix of technology and expert advisory, materially shaping a market-leading business where your contribution will be highly visible and valued.

A Day in the Life

New Business Development: You'll identify, qualify, develop, and close opportunities across pharma and biotech - connecting with potential clients virtually and aconferences and events, building pipeline, and leading sales conversations that connect Mystra's capabilities to real research challenges.

Client Relationships: You'll develop an understanding of your clients' R&D and data priorities, and build trusted relationships with scientific and commercial stakeholders and keeping them engaged throughout the sales process and beyond.

Market Awareness: You'll stay curious about what's happening across the life sciences landscape, bringing insights from client conversations back to the team and helping evolve our positioning and messaging.

Sales Operations: You'll keep your pipeline accurate and up to date in our CRM, contributing to forecasting and reporting that helps the wider team plan effectively.

Internal Collaboration: You'll work closely with Consulting, Product, Science, and Marketing colleagues - sharing what you're hearing in the market and helping ensure our sales approach reflects genuine customer need.



Who You Are

 - You have experience in SaaS or enterprise technology sales into pharma or biotech - you understand how these organisations buy and what matters to them.

 - You've engaged with research or R&D teams inside pharma or biotech companies and know how to build credibility in a scientific environment.

 - You have an interest in genetics or genomics - whether from your academic background or professional experience - and you're confident talking about what advanced genetic data can do.

 - You communicate clearly and enthusiastically when presenting to a mixed scientific and commercial audience.

 - You're proactive, organised, and genuinely excited by the prospect of building something - this is a role where your energy and initiative will make a real difference.

 - A degree in life sciences, business, or a related field is expected.



Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.


COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Salary: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure top-of-market compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer - 3 month FTC
source_jobs.description:
Location: Oxford or London (Hybrid)

 

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

 


ROLE PURPOSE

Your mission is to maximise the commercial team's efficiency so they spend the vast majority of their time in high-value conversations - not researching, not updating CRM, not writing cold emails from scratch. That's your domain.

 - Scanning the biotech and life sciences landscape for funding rounds, leadership changes, conference activity, and partnership news - and translating those signals into actionable briefs for the sales team.

 - Use tools to draft scientifically credible, trigger-specific outreach at scale - emails and LinkedIn messages that are personal.

 - Owning HubSpot end-to-end: workflows, data hygiene, reporting, and optimisation.

 - Building and running outreach sequences via Apollo and LinkedIn Sales Navigator, with smart timing and follow-up logic.

 - Coordinating conference outreach - mapping attendees, scheduling pre/post-event follow-ups, making sure no warm lead goes cold.

 - Documenting what works. You'll build simple playbooks and train the team on the tools and tactics that are moving the needle.

 - Qualifying inbound and self-sourced leads against our Ideal Customer Profile - keeping the pipeline focused on the highest-relevance biotech and genetics accounts.


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

You'll thrive here if you're someone who:

 - Has strong, hands-on HubSpot experience - you know it well enough to tune it, not just use it.

 - Writes with clarity and credibility - you can translate complex science into compelling commercial messaging.

 - Is genuinely curious about the biotech and life sciences landscape - funding cycles, R&D trends, the names that matter.

 - Has good instincts for what's signal vs noise - and the judgement to prioritise accordingly.

 - Is comfortable with Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and AI-assisted content tools.

 - Has worked in complex B2B sales environments - life sciences, data, or SaaS backgrounds are particularly relevant.


EVEN BETTER IF YOU HAVE

 - Experience owning or contributing to sales enablement programmes or playbooks.

 - Confidence engaging with senior commercial and scientific stakeholders.

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted research workflows - prompting, output editing, quality control.



Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.

 


COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous Learning: Including external courses and a wide library of L&D materials, because your growth is our success.


WELLBEING & TIME OFF

 - Holiday: 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays, plus an extra 3-day company-wide shutdown at year-end.

 - Financial & Health Security: Robust benefits including a market-leading pension scheme, comprehensive private health insurance for you and your family with NO excess, critical illness, and life assurance.

 - Enhanced Leave: Enhanced paid family leave to support all new parents.


W

source_jobs.title: Partnerships Manager
source_jobs.description:
Location: Oxford or London (Hybrid)

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

Role Purpose

As our Partnership Manager, you'll be the person our life science partners rely on - the trusted scientific peer who keeps complex projects on track, relationships strong, and discoveries moving forward. You'll lead your own portfolio of partnerships independently while contributing to some of our most strategic accounts, sitting at the intersection of science, commercial impact, and human connection.

This is a role for someone who gets genuine satisfaction from seeing a partner succeed - and who understands that the work we do together could one day change how diseases are found, treated, and prevented.

A Day in the Life

Client Delivery: You'll own the day-to-day running of your partnerships - keeping stakeholders engaged, projects on course, and ensuring every deliverable reflects the quality our partners expect and deserve.

Project & Financial Ownership: You'll hold the reins on timelines, budgets, and resources for your accounts, bringing the kind of rigour and care that keeps things running smoothly even when they get complicated.

Strategic Growth: You'll develop a genuine understanding of your partners' goals - spotting the moments where Genomics can offer more, and shaping those conversations with sensitivity and scientific credibility.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: You'll work closely with Science, Finance, Legal, and Commercial colleagues, bringing people together around a shared purpose and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

Practice Building: Beyond your accounts, you'll contribute to initiatives that strengthen how our Technology Enabled Life Sciences practice operates - helping us get better at what we do, together.

Who You Are

 - You have a strong scientific foundation - ideally a PhD or MSc in genetics, genomics, or a related life science - and you can hold your own in a conversation with a statistical geneticist or a pharma R&D lead.

 - You've worked in a client-facing role in life sciences, biotech, or healthcare - whether in consulting, alliance management, or a similar capacity - and you know what it takes to earn and keep a partner's trust.

 - You're a natural organiser who takes real ownership of your work, keeping budgets, timelines, and stakeholders aligned without losing sight of the bigger picture.

 - You notice opportunities others might miss - when a partner's challenge maps onto something Genomics does well, you know how to shape that conversation thoughtfully.

 - You thrive in collaborative, multidisciplinary environments and bring warmth and clarity to every team you work with.



Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.


COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous Learning: Including external courses and a wide library of L&D materials, because your growth is our success.


WELLBEING & TIME OFF

 - Holiday: 25 days annual leave,

source_jobs.title: Healthcare Product Owner
source_jobs.description:
Location: London or Oxford (Hybrid)

The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

 


THE ROLE

We're looking for a Product Owner to help shape and run our healthcare product delivery team's backlog - turning discovery and strategic intent into clear, well-specified, buildable work.

This is hands-on and delivery-focused. You'll keep the backlog refined and build-ready, write the user stories and acceptance criteria the team works from, and help maintain the requirements board that delivery depends on - across a cross-functional team of Engineering, Science, Lab, Medical, Regulatory, Quality and Design. You'll deliver reliably and independently on well-defined work, growing your judgement on prioritisation and scope with support from senior product and delivery colleagues, and you'll take the initiative to spot improvements and raise risks as you go.

Because we build software as a medical device, the work has to be evidenced, controlled and safe to the standard the product's classification demands. You don't need to know it all on day one - but you'll be keen to build that regulatory know-how fast.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Keep the backlog refined and build-ready so the team always has clear, well-specified work to pull from.

 - Write clear user stories and acceptance criteria, working with each function to capture what they need.

 - Help maintain the requirements board, keeping cross-functional and regulatory requirements current and traceable with support from the wider team.

 - Review delivered work against acceptance criteria and the definition of done, seeking input on the calls that aren't clear-cut.

 - Contribute to discovery as a delivery voice, flagging build, regulatory and evidence implications early.

 - Spot risk during refinement and route it to the right owner so nothing slips through.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Some experience in a Product Owner, Business Analyst or similar delivery role - one that has touched regulated software within a medical environment (medical device, IVD or SaMD a real plus). You will also build your knowledge of standards like IEC 62304 and ISO 13485 and how they shape what gets built

 - Solid on writing clear user stories and acceptance criteria, and comfortable gathering requirements from technical and non-technical colleagues alike.

 - A scientific background, or an understanding of technical scientific products.

 - Reliable and organised, with sound judgement about when to make the call and when to seek input.

 - Clear, consistent communication - you explain things so others can act on them, and keep people informed and aligned.
   
    

Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.

Compensation & Growth

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous Learning: Including external courses and a wide library of L&D materials, because your growth is our success.


WELLBEING & TIME OFF

 - Holiday: 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays, plus an extra 3-day comp

Location: Hybrid-based out of our RTP office, although this role will be to cover the West Coast market.

Internal Influence: You'll carry significant internal weight as the voice of the customer — shaping how Product, Science, and Marketing think about the market and ensuring our commercial approach is always grounded in genuine value.



Who You Are

 - You have a substantial track record of senior-level SaaS, digital health, or enterprise technology sales into pharma or biotech, with demonstrable success closing complex, high-value deals.

 - You have deep experience engaging with R&D and research departments inside major pharmaceutical companies, and you navigate senior scientific and commercial stakeholders with ease.

 - You have a strong working knowledge of genetics or genomics — enough to speak credibly and compellingly about what advanced genetic analytics can unlock for drug discovery.

 - You think strategically about markets, clients, and positioning, and bring that perspective into everything you do.

 - You're a self-directed, senior operator who thrives in a fast-paced scale-up environment and doesn't need a roadmap to find the right path.

 - A degree in life sciences, business, or a related field is expected; an advanced degree is a genuine asset.

Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: London (Hybrid Working)



The Mission: Why We Exist

Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio combining large-scale genetic and health data with proprietary analytics to accelerate drug discovery and advance predictive, preventative healthcare. We are united by a single vision to help people live longer, healthier lives, using the power of genomics.

Genomics aims to help people live longer, healthier lives in two ways: super-charging drug discovery and development for novel treatments with our AI-enabled advanced genetic analytics platform, and by helping people understand their personal risk of common chronic diseases through polygenic risk scores - giving doctors and health systems the chance to get the right people into the right prevention, screening and treatment programmes at the right time.

Role Purpose

As our Business Development Lead, you'll be a driven, scientifically-minded commercial professional helping to grow Mystra’s penetration into the pharma and biotech market. You'll own your pipeline, build real relationships with client R&D and data teams, and play a hands-on role in selling Mystra and our specialised consultancy services.

Operating in one of the most exciting growth areas in life sciences, this is a brilliant opportunity for someone with commercial ambition who is ready to take genuine ownership. You will help clients solve complex challenges through a mix of technology and expert advisory, materially shaping a market-leading business where your contribution will be highly visible and valued.



A Day in the Life

New Business Development: You'll identify, qualify, develop, and close opportunities across pharma and biotech - connecting with potential clients virtually and aconferences and events, building pipeline, and leading sales conversations that connect Mystra's capabilities to real research challenges.

Client Relationships: You'll develop an understanding of your clients' R&D and data priorities, and build trusted relationships with scientific and commercial stakeholders and keeping them engaged throughout the sales process and beyond.

Market Awareness: You'll stay curious about what's happening across the life sciences landscape, bringing insights from client conversations back to the team and helping evolve our positioning and messaging.

Sales Operations: You'll keep your pipeline accurate and up to date in our CRM, contributing to forecasting and reporting that helps the wider team plan effectively.

Internal Collaboration: You'll work closely with Consulting, Product, Science, and Marketing colleagues - sharing what you're hearing in the market and helping ensure our sales approach reflects genuine customer need.



Who You Are

 - You have experience in SaaS or enterprise technology sales into pharma or biotech - you understand how these organisations buy and what matters to them.

 - You've engaged with research or R&D teams inside pharma or biotech companies and know how to build credibility in a scientific environment.

 - You have an interest in genetics or genomics - whether from your academic background or professional experience - and you're confident talking about what advanced genetic data can do.

 - You communicate clearly and enthusiastically when you're presenting to a mixed scientific and commercial audience.

 - You're proactive, organised, and genuinely excited by the prospect of building something - this is a role where your energy and initiative will make a real difference.

 - A degree in life sciences, business, or a related field is expected.



Your Package

We are committed to providing a transparent, supportive, and rewarding work environment.


COMPENSATION & GROWTH

 - Competitive Reward: Salaries are externally benchmarked annually to ensure market-aligned compensation.

 - Clear Career Path: A straightforward, open progression framework means you'll always know the path to promotion and how to achieve your next career goal.

 - Continuous L

notes: Genomics is a science-led transatlantic TechBio with a mix of business development, scientific, and technical roles.
